steps.title: Rearrange to standard form
steps.content:
Start with $2x^2 - 2 = 2x + 3$. Move all terms to the left:
$$2x^2 - 2x - 5 = 0$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $2$ — check: $2(2)^2 - 2 = 6$ but $2(2)+3 = 7$, so $6 \ne 7$. Wrong.
  • B) $-\sqrt{11}$ — not of the form $\frac{1 \pm \sqrt{11}}{2}$; substituting gives $2(11) - 2 = 20$ vs $-2\sqrt{11}+3 \approx -3.63$. Wrong.
  • C) $\frac{1}{2} + \sqrt{11}$ — differs from $\frac{1+\sqrt{11}}{2}$; the $\sqrt{11}$ is not halved, so it does not satisfy the equation. Wrong.
  • D) $\frac{1 + \sqrt{11}}{2}$ — exactly one of the two roots derived above. Correct.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
